title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: From the 1,364 whole-genome sequences, we identified 10,929,118 somatic mutations, which include 8,935,132 single nucleotide variants (SNVs), 1,785,446 indels and 208,540 structural variants with a substantial variation in their burden per sample (Extended Data Fig. 1 and Supplementary Figs. 2 and 3 present the full oncogrid plots; interactive exploration of the genomic variants is also possible at https://open.cancervision.com).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The median tumour mutational burden (TMB) was 4,742 (interquartile range (IQR) 2,392–8,842), with subtype-specific medians as follows: luminal A, 2,182 (IQR 1,628–2,841); luminal B, 4,027 (IQR 2,376–7,392); HER2-enriched, 6,737 (IQR 4,076–14,213); and basal-like, 8,360 (IQR 5,296–11,504).
outcome: Basal-like tumours had the highest TMB,

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: By applying the IntOGen pipeline (Ref: 17), we identified 41 breast cancer driver genes (Methods), including 4 novel driver genes
context: in addition to classical drivers such as TP53, PIK3CA and GATA3 (Ref: 3)

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: For example, BCL11B, which encodes a transcription factor that is involved in chromatic remodelling, gene regulation and mammary stem cell self-renewal (Ref: 18,19), was mutated in 23 patients at much higher frequency than random expectation.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Similarly, RREB1, which encodes a zinc finger transcription factor that regulates Ras and TGFβ signalling and directly binds the p53 promoter (Ref: 20-22), was truncated in 26 patients,
outcome: suggesting that its disruption could affect oncogenic pathways.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: (both were mutated in five patients)
outcome: Our data also suggest that RAF1 (Ref: 23) and SPECC1 (Ref: 24) (both were mutated in five patients) act as tumour suppressors, with potential roles in tumour initiation and progression.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Tumours with TP53 mutations frequently exhibited genomic instability (Ref: 25), characterized by higher ploidy and increased homologous recombination DNA repair deficiency (HRD) for double-strand DNA breaks (Extended Data Fig. 3a,b).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: TP53 mutations were positively associated with the level of intratumour genetic heterogeneity (calculated by mutant allele tumour heterogeneity (MATH) scoring with tumour WGS (Ref: 4)), particularly in basal-like subtypes (Extended Data Fig. 3c shows MATH scores associated with different TP53 mutations),
outcome: indicating more active late-stage mutagenesis and emergence of potent subclones in TP53-mutant tumours.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Notably, higher MATH scores were associated with poorer overall survival, particularly in TP53-mutant tumours (hazard ratio 1.66, 95% confidence interval 1.08–2.74; P = 0.048; Extended Data Fig. 3d,e),
outcome: suggesting that intratumoural heterogeneity, as measured by MATH score, is associated with clinical outcomes and deserves further investigation as a potential prognostic factor.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The prognostic effect of the MATH score was further demonstrated in the METABRIC cohort of 1,670 participants (Ref: 26) (Extended Data Fig. 3f and Supplementary Discussion 2).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: To systematically explore the mutational processes that shape alterations in breast cancer genomes,
outcome: we performed a comprehensive mutational signature analysis (Ref: 2) (Methods, Supplementary Discussion 3 and Supplementary Fig. 4).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: We identified various signatures, including 17 SNV signatures, 9 insertion–deletion mutation (indel) signatures and 6 structural variant signatures (Fig. 2a; reference mutational signatures are available at https://cancer.sanger.ac.uk/signatures/).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Signatures with clock-like properties, such as SBS5, ID1 and ID2, were present in nearly all samples, and accounted for a substantial proportion (more than 10%) of somatic mutations.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: By contrast, some mutational signatures contributed to a high number of mutations in only a subset of samples,
outcome: suggesting hyper-mutagenicity that was restricted to the affected tumours.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: For examples, mutational processes attributable to polymerase epsilon exonuclease domain mutations (SBS10a and SBS10b) and defective DNA mismatch repair (SBS21 and SBS44) were observed in four cases with an extremely high TMB (more than 100,000; Supplementary Fig. 5).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Mutational signatures associated with HRD (Ref: 3,27), including SBS3, SBS8, ID6, SV3 and SV5, are of particular interest,
outcome: as they are potential WGS-based biomarkers for poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase inhibitors (Fig. 2b,c).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The combined estimated HRD scores (Methods) showed a clear bimodal distribution (Fig. 2c), identifying 315 cases as HRD in the CUBRICS cohort (23.1%, 315 out of 1,364; Fig. 2d and Supplementary Fig. 6).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Although it is highly enriched in the basal-like cancers (72.2%, 182 out of 252), HRD was also present in other subtypes (Fig. 2d).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Out of the 315 cases identified as HRD, we identified causal pathogenic variants on genes in the homologous recombination pathway in 130 cases (41.3%, 130 out of 315), including 90 with germline defects (28.6%, 90 out of 315) and 40 (17.8%, 40 out of 225) with only somatically acquired defects (Supplementary Discussion 4, Supplementary Fig. 7 and Supplementary Table 2).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Although germline pathogenic variants predominantly inactivated BRCA1 and BRCA2, somatic mutations most frequently truncated RAD51B, primarily by genomic rearrangements rather than point mutations (Fig. 2e).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The CUBRICS cohort included 89 patients with triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) with a treatment history of adjuvant chemotherapy containing anthracycline–cyclophosphamide (Supplementary Table 3).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Individuals with HRD (n = 66) exhibited significantly superior disease-free survival compared with those with homologous recombination DNA repair-proficient (HRP) cancer (hazard ratio 0.10, 95% confidence interval 0.02–0.54; P = 0.006; Fig. 2f); this difference was more pronounced than in a previous study with a targeted panel-based assay (Ref: 28),
outcome: highlighting the potential of WGS for more specific clinical evaluation of HRD.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: APOBEC-associated mutational signatures (SBS2 and SBS13; Fig. 2b) were widespread in breast cancers, contributing more than 10% of somatic SNVs in 633 samples.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of these, 12 samples were hypermutators with APOBEC-associated mutational processes, with TMB of more than 50,000.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: A germline deletion involving APOBEC3A and APOBEC3B (APOBEC3A/B), which effectively generates APOBEC3A–APOBEC3B fusion transcripts, has been suggested to predispose to APOBEC-mediated hypermutations (Ref: 29) (Extended Data Fig. 4a).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, the population allele frequency of the germline deletion was 31.8% in our cohort (present in 736 individuals, 131 being homozygous and 605 heterozygous; Fig. 2g and Extended Data Fig. 4b), significantly higher than the frequency in the European population (Ref: 29) (31.8% versus 8.5%; P < 0.001 by two-sided chi-square test),

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Contrary to previous reports (Ref: 30,31), the deletion was not substantially enriched in patients with breast cancer compared with the background Korean population (51.0%, 844 out of 1,654 individuals, with 150 being homozygous and 694 heterozygous; P = 0.276 by two-sided chi-square test; Fig. 2g),
outcome: suggesting that it is not a strong cancer-predisposing germline variant.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: However, carriers exhibited reduced APOBEC3B expression (Extended Data Fig. 4c), significantly higher TMB (median 4,325 in wild type versus median 5,148 in carriers; P < 0.001; Fig. 2h) and APOBEC-associated signatures (SBS2, SBS13 and ID9; Extended Data Fig. 4d) in cancers.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In addition, wild-type individuals were substantially depleted in the APOBEC hypermutator cancers (10.0% in the top 30 cases versus 46.9% in others; P < 0.001 by two-sided Fisher’s exact test; Fig. 2i).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: APOBEC-deletion carriers showed an enrichment of APOBEC-associated mutations in the YpTpCpN context than in the RpTpCpN context (where Y represents pyrimidine, R represents purine and the mutated cytosine is underlined; Fig. 2h).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: To determine the structural variation landscape,
context: A key mutational process in cancer is structural variation, which acts to amplify, delete or reorder chromosomal material at scales that range from single genes to entire chromosomes (Ref: 32).
outcome: we generated a DNA interaction map between 5-Mb segments across the entire genome (Fig. 3a), along with large-scale copy number amplification patterns (CNAs; G-score).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of the 208,540 structural variation events, local intrachromosomal (<5 Mb) rearrangements were predominant (162,988 out of 208,540, 78.2%), but reshuffling of genomic materials between distal intrachromosomal or interchromosomal regions were also observed (45,266 out of 208,540, 21.7%).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Some of the rearrangements were observed more frequently than random. For example, 15 breast cancers carried an interchromosomal translocation between chromosomes 8 and 11, which brought CCND1, and ZNF703 and FGFR1 (ZNF703/FGFR1) into close proximity (mostly luminal B type; 73.3%, 11 out of 15; Fig. 3a, top left).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, the interaction was observed in lung cancer (Ref: 33), and was also reported to be associated with reduced benefit from aromatase inhibitors in metastatic breast cancer (Ref: 34).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Consistent with these findings, tumours with these rearrangements exhibited distinct transcriptomic profiles, including upregulation of tumour progression-related pathways such as TNF, TGFβ signalling and epithelial–mesenchymal transition, as well as metabolic reprogramming (Extended Data Fig. 5).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Another frequent interchromosomal translocation involved the ERBB2 locus on chromosome 17 and a locus on chromosome 20 (observed in 30 participants),
context: where breast cancer super-enhancers were enriched (Ref: 35) (Fig. 3a, top right).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Tumours with extragenic structural variants in the vicinity (within 1 Mb) showed significantly higher ERBB2 gene expression levels than their gene copy number (log2 fold change >1, q-value <0.10; Fig. 3d). They often carried extragenic structural variants in the vicinity (within 1 Mb) with a cancer cell fraction (CCF) of approximately 1 (Fig. 3e), and distal breakpoints significantly enriched near breast cancer-specific super-enhancers (Ref: 35) (Fig. 3f).
context: Structural variations can influence transcription by disrupting enhancer–promoter interactions, chromatin topology and long-range regulatory elements (Ref: 36).
outcome: Our observation overall suggests a possible enhancer-hijacking mechanism for transcriptional activation in breast cancer genomes.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In addition, we found gene truncation events associated with structural variation. A total of 28 genes, including the well-established tumour suppressors RB1, PTEN and RUNX1, exhibited significant downregulation due to gene-truncating structural variants,
outcome: reinforcing their tumour suppressor role in breast cancer (Fig. 3g).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: we identified several recurrent fusion events (Fig. 3h and Extended Data Figs. 6 and 7).
context: Although recurrent fusion oncogenes are considered rare in breast cancer (Ref: 37),

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In luminal-type breast cancers, frequent fusion genes were MIPOL1–TTC6 (n = 9), CEP112–PRKCA (n = 6) and CCDC170–ESR1 (n = 6). In basal-like breast cancers, BCL2L14–ETV6 (n = 12), AGO2–PTK2 (n = 6) and BRD4–NOTCH3 (n = 6) were recurrent.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, CCDC170–ESR1 has been implicated in endocrine therapy resistance and metastasis in breast cancers (Ref: 38) and BCL2L14–ETV6 has been linked to increased invasiveness and paclitaxel resistance (Ref: 39).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The fraction of variant-supporting reads suggests that most of the fusion events are clonal or near-clonal,

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In the gene-level analysis, NRG1, which encodes a small ligand of ERBB family kinase receptors, was frequently affected by 166 structural variants in 110 cases (8%, 110 out of 1,364; Extended Data Fig. 8; top 2).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Although gene disruption was the most frequent outcome (72.9%, 121 out of 166 structural variants), oncogenic fusions were sometimes formed with various partner genes (Ref: 40) (n = 17; Fig. 3i), collectively sixfold more frequently than previous reports (Ref: 41) (1.25% versus 0.2%).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, the EGF-like domain of NRG1 was retained in all 17 fusion cases,

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: To explore these events in breast cancers,
context: Focal amplification, which increases the copy number of short-segment DNA (typically up to 3 Mb in size, with a copy number gain greater than 3 compared with flanking regions), is a prominent signature in many human cancers (Ref: 42,43).
outcome: we calculated the recurrence score of focal amplifications across the genome, referred to as F-score (Fig. 4a).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The most prominent peaks were observed at chromosome 8 (chr. 8):35–40 Mb, chr. 11:65–70 Mb and chr. 17:35–40 Mb, which included ZNF703 (chr. 8; n = 124; 9.1%) and FGFR1 (chr. 8; n = 107; 7.8%), CCND1 (chr. 11; n = 108; 7.9%) and ERBB2 (chr. 17; n = 255; 18.7%), respectively (Fig. 4b and Extended Data Fig. 9a).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Notably, ZNF703 exhibited higher amplification levels than FGFR1 on chromosome 8,
outcome: suggesting its oncogenic role in breast cancer.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Gene expression levels of the oncogenes positively correlated with gene copy number in a dose-dependent manner (Extended Data Fig. 9b).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Notably, ERBB2 expression exhibited mutual exclusivity with the other oncogenes,
outcome: suggesting a redundant oncogenic mechanism between ERBB2 and the other three genes in breast tumorigenesis (Extended Data Fig. 9c).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: However, CCND1 and ZNF703/FGFR1 loci displayed a positive correlation in RNA expression,
outcome: in line with their co-rearrangement as previously discussed (Fig. 3a).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, more than 40% of the ERBB2, FGFR1, ZNF703 and CCND1 focal amplifications were formed by extrachromosomal DNA (ecDNA) (Fig. 4c),
outcome: suggesting that ecDNA is the primary mechanism of focal amplification

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Overall, 387 cancer tissues (28.4%, 387 out of 1,364) carried ecDNA, whose location was tightly correlated with F-score (Fig. 4a).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: In our cohort, as expected, HER2 IHC correlated with, but did not completely overlap with PAM50 subtypes or ERBB2 gene copy number gains (Fig. 4d).
context: Immunohistochemistry (IHC) targeting HER2 protein, encoded by ERBB2, is a gold standard technique for clinical detection of HER2-positive breast cancers (Ref: 45).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: For example, ecDNA-derived focal amplification of ERBB2 (copy number 68) was observed in a case with HER2 IHC 0 (Extended Data Fig. 9d).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Conversely, we also observed a case with HER2 IHC 3+ without obvious ERBB2 amplification (Extended Data Fig. 9e). The PAM50 subtype of this case was not HER2-enriched but luminal B, with ERBB2 transcription markedly lower (transcripts per million (TPM) = 43.5) than typically observed in HER2 IHC 3+ tumours (mean TPM = 692.0), but similar to HER2 IHC 0 tumours (Extended Data Fig. 9e).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Then, we evaluated whether these genome and transcriptome data can predict treatment responses in HER2-positive cancer.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Out of 75 individuals with HER2-positive tumours who received neoadjuvant TCHP regimens (docetaxel (microtubule inhibitor), carboplatin (platinum intercalator), trastuzumab and pertuzumab (anti-HER2 inhibitors)), 38 showed pathologic complete response (pCR) (Fig. 4e and Supplementary Table 4).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Although cases without pCR (n = 37) were characterized by a higher prevalence of luminal subtypes (P < 0.001, two-sided Fisher’s exact text), hormone receptor positivity (P = 0.001, two-sided chi-square test) and PIK3CA mutations (P = 0.003, two-sided chi-square test), cases with pCR (n = 38) exhibited significantly higher ERBB2 expression (P < 0.001, two-sided Student’s t-test) and a greater frequency of ERBB2 focal amplification (P = 0.001, two-sided Fisher’s exact test; Extended Data Fig. 9f–h (external validation in the TransNEO cohort (Ref: 46))).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Although HER2 IHC 3+ was a sensitive biomarker for pCR (36 out of 38, 94.7%), ERBB2 copy number (n ≥ 33; Extended Data Fig. 9i,j) demonstrated higher precision (30 out of 38, 78.9%; Fig. 4f).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Overall, ERBB2 copy number provided a complementary performance, with higher prediction in specificity, precision, and likelihood ratio (Fig. 4g).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Notably, in our cohort, tumours with ERBB2 copy number of 33 or more were significantly more likely to be ecDNA-positive (P = 0.003; Fig. 4f, right); however, ERBB2 amplification by ecDNA was not associated with pCR rates (P = 0.540).
context: Of note, ecDNA is generally associated with poor prognosis across multiple cancer types (Ref: 47).
outcome: Our observation suggests that absolute copy number. rather than the amplification mechanism, may be a more relevant predictor of therapeutic response. It also indicates that ERBB2-amplifying ecDNA may be an actionable target for anti-HER2 therapy.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Further, we observed a significant enrichment of chromothripsis events, a complex genomic rearrangement with catastrophic chromosomal shattering and erroneous reassembly, in cases with pCR (Ref: 48) (71.1% versus 43.2%; Fig. 4e,h and Supplementary Fig. 8).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
subsection: Clinical effect of ERBB2 focal gain
main_content: Adding chromothripsis to cancer stratification improved the precision and specificity of predicting responses to TCHP (Fig. 4g, Extended Data Fig. 9k and Supplementary Discussion 5).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Along with focal amplifications, long-segmental amplifications (typically larger than 1 Mb), often chromosome- or arm-level alterations, were common in breast cancers (Fig. 3a).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The timing analysis with the burden of co-amplified, pre-amplification somatic base substitutions yielded the acquisition timing of the frequent long-segmental CNAs in breast cancer in molecular time (Ref: 49) (Extended Data Fig. 10).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Most of the recurrent long-segmental CNAs were acquired by 20% of molecular time, when recent common ancestral cells of a cancer emerge, which should be decades earlier than tumour diagnosis.
outcome: This implies that acquisition of the long-segmental CNAs is an early evolutionary event in breast cancer, which is presumably acquired in early puberty,

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Our findings also suggest that full neoplastic transformation can take decades from the first event of genomic instability.
outcome: Our findings also suggest that full neoplastic transformation can take decades from the first event of genomic instability.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The most common CNAs occurred at 8q21.13, with a relative timing of 0.15 (IQR 0.08–0.25) and a median copy number gain of 5.5 (IQR 4.0–8.0).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: This amplification was associated with poor overall survival (hazard ratio 1.67, 95% confidence interval 1.09–2.55; P = 0.019).
context: This region contains the MYC oncogene, which has been linked to treatment resistance and poor survival across various solid tumours, including breast cancer (Ref: 51).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, CNAs of 9q23 (n = 40), occurring at a relative timing of 0.13 (IQR 0.09–0.25), were enriched in basal-like cancers and exhibited poor overall survival in the subtype (hazard ratio 2.45, 95% confidence interval 1.16–5.18; P = 0.019; Fig. 4i and Supplementary Table 5).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: External validation in the METABRIC cohort confirmed this trend (Ref: 52) (Extended Data Fig. 11a,b and Supplementary Table 6).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The 9p23 gain was associated with 823 differentially expressed genes genome-wide (282 upregulated and 531 downregulated; Extended Data Fig. 11c),
context: including PSIP1, which has been reported to be negatively correlated with survival of patients with TNBC (Ref: 53).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Late-stage CNA acquisitions were more frequently observed in HRD breast cancers (P < 0.001; Extended Data Fig. 11d,e).
outcome: This implies that the rate of large-segmental CNAs is accelerated when a tumour cell acquires the HRD phenotype.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: For instance, in one case with HRD (no. 635; Fig. 4j), the rate of CNA acquisition was substantially increased from about 75% of the molecular time,
outcome: which we speculate is the time of complete inactivation of the homologous recombination-mediated DNA repair pathway.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: By contrast, in another case with HRD (no. 703; Fig. 4k), there was no such late CNA acquisition stage.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: The cancer developed talazoparib resistance after ten months of the second-line palliative treatment.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In line with the resistance, post-therapy cancer genome exhibited an 8-bp deletion in the vicinity of the germline 1-bp deletion in BRCA1,
outcome: which is likely to have converted an HRD tumour back to HRP (Fig. 4l).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: From the timing analysis (Fig. 4k, bottom),
outcome: we believe that the rescuing mutation could be acquired in about 50% of the molecular time in one of the cells in the primary cancer tissue, which could lead to talazoparib resistance.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: we explored the potential utility of whole-genome results
outcome: by integrating genomic profiles and clinical records.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: First, we observed that an increased MATH score, was associated with worse progression-free survival (PFS) in first-line anti-HER2 therapies against HER2-positive breast cancer.
context: which is indicative of higher intratumoural heterogeneity,

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In our cohort, 45 patients who received the therapy showed a median PFS of 1.08 years (range 0.14–9.96 years; as of 15 November 2023; Fig. 5a and Supplementary Table 7).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Individuals with a high MATH score (≥40; cut-off with the median MATH score) exhibited worse PFS compared to those with a MATH score below 40 (hazard ratio 2.95, 95% confidence interval 1.48–5.91; P = 0.002; Fig. 5b,c).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Similarly, we identified high TMB, and HRD scores were associated with worse PFS in CDK4/6 inhibitor plus endocrine therapy,
context: a first-line palliative treatment recently developed for hormone receptor-positive breast cancer (Ref: 54)

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In a total of 57 patients receiving the regimens, individuals with a high TMB (≥1.7 per Mb, cut-off with the median TMB) exhibited poor PFS (hazard ratio 2.55, 95% confidence interval 1.26–5.16; P = 0.009; Fig. 5d,e).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Thirteen out of the 57 patients exhibited HRD, 85% of whom experienced progression, resulting in a shorter PFS than those with HRP (hazard ratio 4.20, 95% confidence interval 1.91–9.25; P < 0.001; Fig. 5f and Supplementary Table 8).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Multivariate Cox proportional hazards analysis indicated HRD as the single most significant factor predicting favourable PFS to the treatment (hazard ratio 10.20, 95% confidence interval 1.68–61.80; P = 0.012; Fig. 5g),
outcome: warranting validation in larger, prospective studies.

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: In gene set enrichment analysis (GSEA), these HRD tumours showed significant enrichment of pathways related to cell cycle progression, DNA repair and mitotic checkpoint activation,
outcome: suggesting that these tumours may exhibit increased reliance on cell cycle regulatory mechanisms (Extended Data Fig. 12).

title: Whole-genome landscapes of 1,364 breast cancers
main_content: Of note, PTEN mutations were exclusively present in individuals who experienced progression on the treatment,
outcome: suggesting that they had a negative impact on the treatment (P = 0.042, two-sided Fisher’s exact test; Fig. 5d).
